message:
feat(core): Add dinucleotide bias decomposition analysis module

Implement a lightweight PCA-based analysis of dinucleotide frequencies
for comparing compositional signatures across multiple phage genomes
without requiring external numerical libraries.

New module: packages/core/src/bias-decomposition.ts (144 lines)

Core Algorithm:
- computeDinucleotideFrequencies(seq): Extracts normalized 16-element
  frequency vector for all dinucleotide combinations (AA, AC, AG, AT,
  CA, CC, CG, CT, GA, GC, GG, GT, TA, TC, TG, TT). Skips non-ACGT
  characters gracefully.

- decomposeBias(rows): Performs 2-component PCA on dinucleotide vectors:
  1. Centers the matrix by subtracting feature means
  2. Computes covariance matrix (n×16 → 16×16)
  3. Extracts PC1 via power iteration (64 iterations)
  4. Deflates covariance and extracts PC2
  5. Projects samples onto PC1/PC2 for 2D visualization

Types Exported:
- Dinucleotide: Union of 16 dinucleotide strings
- BiasComponent: { id, loadings[], explained } for each PC
- BiasProjection: { name, coords: [number, number] } per sample
- BiasDecomposition: Complete result with components, projections, means

Mathematical Implementation:
- centerMatrix(): Computes column means and centers data
- covariance(): Builds sample covariance matrix with n-1 normalization
- powerIteration(): Finds dominant eigenvector/eigenvalue pair
- project2(): Projects centered data onto two principal components

Design Rationale:
- Pure TypeScript with no external dependencies (no numpy/scipy)
- Power iteration chosen for simplicity and small matrix sizes (16×16)
- 64 iterations ensures convergence for well-conditioned covariance
- Deflation enables sequential extraction of orthogonal components
- Explained variance computed as |eigenvalue|/sum(|eigenvalues|)

This enables comparison of genome composition across phage collections,
useful for identifying taxonomic groupings, detecting anomalous genomes,
and visualizing the dinucleotide "fingerprint" diversity in a dataset.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
